## Riot Relay 1.4.4

### Removed
- The experimental custom-branded installer from 1.4.3 has been fully removed (code, build steps, and the previously published asset). Installation goes back to just the NSIS EXE and MSI installers.

### Added
- A new **Uninstall Riot Relay** section in Settings › Security. It launches the real Windows uninstaller from inside the app, with a choice to keep or permanently delete your saved accounts, sessions, and settings first. Only available in the installed NSIS EXE build; MSI installs are still removed from Windows Settings › Apps.

### Fixed
- **Session capture after login was silently skipped.** After a verified sign-in, Riot Relay tries to save the new session in the background so the next switch to that account is instant. That background check was verifying identity through Riot's chat endpoint, which only becomes ready several seconds after login — so the check almost always timed out and quietly gave up without saving anything, even though the account had just verified successfully. It now uses the same fast, reliable identity signal the rest of the app already uses for verification, and gets a longer window to finish.
- **VALORANT past-rank stats were unreadable.** The RR/LP, wins, losses, games, and win-rate figures for each past act were being crammed into a single cramped, non-wrapping line next to the rank name. Past ranks now show the act and rank clearly on their own line, with the stats laid out underneath with proper spacing.
- **Too long a wait between the login form appearing and credentials being typed.** The fallback timer that decides when Riot's login form is "ready enough" to type into was being reset every time the window resized while loading. Riot's newer Client resizes its login window more during hydration, so that timer kept getting pushed back instead of ever firing, turning an intended 6-second fallback into a much longer, open-ended wait. It's now a flat 6-second ceiling that no longer resets.
- **Switching accounts with "launch a game" did not start the game.** Riot changed how game launches work in 2026: requesting a game launch now opens the Riot Client to that game's tab instead of starting the game directly, and the user presses PLAY there. Riot Relay was still waiting up to 25 seconds for a game process that Riot no longer starts on its own, which made a successful, accepted launch request look like it had failed or hung. The wait is now short, and the status message correctly says the Riot Client opened to the game and PLAY needs to be pressed there, instead of implying the launch failed.

### Validation
- All 78 automated tests pass.
- JavaScript syntax checks and diagnostics pass on all changed files.
- Pack build verified.

### Downloads
- **EXE / NSIS (recommended):** normal installation and automatic application updates.
- **MSI:** intended for managed or manual deployment.
- `SHA256SUMS.txt` contains SHA-256 hashes for published assets.

Riot Relay is unofficial community software and is not endorsed by Riot Games.
